text: "Honestly, the thing I care most about is making sure the writing feels real — like, grounded in actual stories from actual people, not just statistics floating in a vacuum. I want donors to feel something, but I never want it to tip into guilt-tripping or emotional manipulation — there's a real difference between moving someone and pressuring them. The tone should feel warm and human, like a conversation, but still professional enough that people trust we're a serious organization. And every piece needs a clear, specific ask at the end — I can't stand when appeals just kind of... trail off without telling people what to do next."
